cultural.modernLegacy: The Seal of Solomon remains a potent symbol in occultism, freemasonry, and popular culture, often associated with magic, protection, and hidden knowledge.

interpretations.description:
The Testament of Solomon
                
                    The "Testament of Solomon," a Greek text from the early centuries CE, provides the most
                    detailed account of the ring's use. It describes how Solomon used the ring to summon and
                    interrogate demons one by one, learning their names, powers, and weaknesses. Each demon
                    was then bound to a specific task in building the Temple or confined so it could harm
                    no one.
                

                Islamic Tradition
                
                    In Islamic tradition, Sulaiman (Solomon) was granted dominion over the jinn by Allah.
                    The ring allowed him to control winds, understand the speech of animals, and command
                    armies of spirits. The Quran describes how jinn worked for Solomon, building palaces
                    and diving for pearls. When he lost the ring briefly to a demon, his kingdom was thrown
                    into chaos until he recovered it.
                

                Western Occultism
                
                    Medieval grimoires such as the "Lesser Key of Solomon" claim to preserve Solomon's magical
                    knowledge. The hexagram or pentagram became known as the "Seal of Solomon" and was used
                    in ceremonial magic to invoke and constrain spirits. This tradition continues in modern
                    occultism and has profoundly influenced Western esotericism.

longDescription:
The Ring of Power That Commanded Demons and Spirits The Seal of Solomon is a legendary magical ring or signet that granted King Solomon
                    power over demons, jinn, and spirits. According to various traditions, the ring was
                    given to Solomon directly by God (or through the archangel Michael) and bore either
                    the ineffable Name of God or a sacred geometric symbol, granting him wisdom and
                    authority over the supernatural world.

extendedContent.title: Origins and Biblical Context
extendedContent.content: While the Bible does not explicitly mention the Seal of Solomon, the figure of King Solomon is depicted as possessing unparalleled wisdom and divine favor. The attribution of magical powers and control over spirits to Solomon developed primarily in post-biblical Jewish and Islamic traditions. Texts such as the Testament of Solomon, a pseudepigraphical work, provide detailed accounts of Solomon's encounters with demons and his use of the ring to subdue them. These narratives emphasize Solomon's unique position as a ruler blessed with both earthly power and spiritual insight. The stories highlight the dangers of unchecked power and the importance of humility in the face of supernatural forces, as Solomon's wisdom is often tested by the cunning of the demons he commands. The biblical portrayal of Solomon's wisdom, combined with later traditions, laid the foundation for the Seal of Solomon's enduring mystique.

extendedContent.title: Variations in Design and Symbolism
extendedContent.content: The design of the Seal of Solomon varies across different cultures and traditions. In some accounts, it is described as a hexagram, formed by two overlapping equilateral triangles, representing the union of opposing forces or the connection between the earthly and divine realms. This form is closely associated with the Star of David in Jewish tradition, though the Seal of Solomon predates its widespread adoption as a Jewish symbol. In other versions, the Seal is depicted as a pentagram, a five-pointed star, which is often associated with protection and the dominion of the spirit over the elements. Regardless of the specific design, the Seal is consistently imbued with symbolic meaning, representing Solomon's wisdom, authority, and ability to control supernatural forces. The variations in design reflect the diverse cultural contexts in which the legend of Solomon and his Seal have been interpreted and adapted over time.

extendedContent.title: The Seal in Magic and Occultism
extendedContent.content: The Seal of Solomon holds a prominent place in magical and occult traditions, often appearing in grimoires and ceremonial magic rituals. It is regarded as a powerful talisman, capable of providing protection, granting control over spirits, and bestowing wisdom upon its possessor. Many magical texts prescribe the use of the Seal in summoning and commanding demons, emphasizing the importance of proper ritual procedures and the potential dangers involved. The Seal is often depicted with inscriptions and symbols that are believed to enhance its power, such as the names of God, angelic sigils, or other magical symbols. In occult circles, the Seal of Solomon is not merely a symbol of power but also a representation of the magician's ability to harness and control the forces of the universe. Its enduring presence in magical traditions underscores its significance as a potent emblem of spiritual authority and esoteric knowledge.

shortDescription: The Ring of Power That Commanded Demons and Spirits
name: Seal of Solomon

sources.name: Testament of Solomon
sources.description: A pseudepigraphal text attributed to King Solomon, detailing his encounters with demons and the construction of the Temple. It is a primary source for many legends surrounding the Seal of Solomon.
sources.title: Testament of Solomon

sources.name: Talmud, Gittin 68a-b
sources.description: A section of the Babylonian Talmud containing a story about Solomon and the demon Asmodeus, a key narrative associated with the Seal's powers and limitations.
sources.title: Talmud, Gittin 68a-b

sources.name: Qur'an, Surah 34:12-13
sources.description: Verses in the Qur'an describing Solomon's (Sulaiman's) ability to command jinn to work for him, reflecting the Islamic perspective on his supernatural powers.
sources.title: Qur'an, Surah 34:12-13

sources.name: 1 Kings 11:1-13
sources.description: A passage from the Hebrew Bible describing Solomon's fall from grace due to his foreign wives and their gods, which some interpretations link to his reliance on magical practices and the Seal.
sources.title: 1 Kings 11:1-13

sources.name: Picatrix (Ghāyat al-Ḥakīm)
sources.description: An 11th-century grimoire of astrological magic, containing references to the Seal of Solomon and its use in summoning spirits and performing magical rituals.
sources.title: Picatrix (Ghāyat al-Ḥakīm)

description: The Seal of Solomon, also known as the Ring of Solomon, is a legendary signet ring attributed to King Solomon, a figure revered in Judaism, Christianity, and Islam. It is often described as a hexagram or pentagram, though the exact design varies across different traditions. The Seal is believed to possess immense power, granting Solomon the ability to command demons, spirits, and other supernatural entities. According to various texts, including the Testament of Solomon, the ring was instrumental in the construction of the First Temple in Jerusalem, as Solomon used it to compel demons to perform labor. The Seal is not merely a symbol of power but also represents Solomon's wisdom and his divinely granted authority over both the earthly and spiritual realms. It is considered an apotropaic symbol, capable of warding off evil and providing protection to its wearer. Over time, the Seal of Solomon has become a significant emblem in occultism, magic, and folklore, symbolizing hidden knowledge, control over the elements, and the union of opposing forces. Its enduring presence in various cultures underscores its profound impact on human imagination and belief systems.

keyMyths.title: Solomon's Acquisition of the Seal
keyMyths.description: The Seal of Solomon, a ring or seal bearing the name of God, was given to King Solomon by God (or sometimes an angel) to aid him in his reign. It granted him power over demons, spirits, and other supernatural entities, enabling him to build the Temple and maintain his kingdom.

keyMyths.title: Construction of the Temple
keyMyths.description: Solomon used the power of the Seal to command demons to labor in the construction of the Temple in Jerusalem. He forced them to quarry stones, transport materials, and perform other tasks, showcasing his dominion over the supernatural realm.

keyMyths.title: Asmodeus and the Seal
keyMyths.description: One prominent narrative involves the demon Asmodeus, who either tricked Solomon into relinquishing the Seal temporarily or was subdued by Solomon using its power. This tale highlights the challenges and vulnerabilities associated with wielding such immense power.

keyMyths.title: Solomon's Fall from Grace
keyMyths.description: Some traditions suggest that Solomon's reliance on the Seal and his interactions with demons led to his spiritual downfall. He became enamored with foreign gods and practices, ultimately losing favor with God due to his misuse of the divine gift.

keyMyths.title: The Seal's Legacy
keyMyths.description: After Solomon's death, the Seal became a symbol of wisdom, authority, and control over the supernatural. It was sought after by magicians, alchemists, and others who desired to harness its power, leading to numerous legends and interpretations throughout history.

associations.name: Hexagram
associations.significance: Represents the union of opposites, heaven and earth, male and female, reflecting Solomon's wisdom in balancing earthly and divine realms.

associations.name: Pentagram
associations.significance: Associated with protection, the five elements, and the dominion of the spirit over matter, reflecting Solomon's control over supernatural forces.

associations.name: Authority
associations.significance: The Seal signifies Solomon's divinely granted authority over demons, spirits, and the natural world.

associations.name: Wisdom
associations.significance: Represents Solomon's legendary wisdom, enabling him to understand and command the forces of the universe.

associations.name: Apotropaic
associations.significance: The Seal wards off evil influences and provides protection against demonic attacks.

associations.name: Djinn
associations.significance: Symbolizes Solomon's ability to control and command djinn, using their powers for construction and other purposes.

cross_cultural_parallels.similarity: Similar function as symbols of authority and used for imprinting marks of ownership or power.
Served as protective amulets with symbolic power, akin to the Seal of Solomon's apotropaic function.
Represents balance and authority, similar to Solomon's Seal symbolizing dominion over spiritual forces.

subtitle: A Ring of Power That Commanded Demons and Spirits

wielders.name: King Solomon
wielders.context: Legendary wielder who used it to control demons and build the Temple.

wielders.name: Various Magicians
wielders.context: Practitioners of ceremonial magic who sought to emulate Solomon's power.
